title: JAPP-KLINGEMANN REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Japp-Klingemann reaction describing diazonium-salt coupling with 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to furnish arylhydrazones through azo intermediates that collapse under acidic or basic conditions.
notes: Key concepts: diazotization of anilines, coupling of arenediazonium salts with active methylene compounds, decarboxylative rearrangement of azo intermediates, and use of arylhydrazones as precursors for Fischer indole synthesis.

entities.notes: Named diazonium coupling family overview.
Arylhydrazones from the Japp-Klingemann reaction are highlighted as key precursors for Fischer indole synthesis.
Characteristic hydrazone product class obtained from diazonium coupling of 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: diazonium coupling of 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Diazonium-coupling manifold that converts 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds into arylhydrazones, often as precursors to Fischer indole synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: HNO2 HBF4 | O=NO.F[B-](F)(F)F | Reagent | O=NO.F[B-](F)(F)F | Reagent | 1. NaNO2, HCl, HBF4 | [Na+].[O-][N]=O.Cl.F[B-](F)(F)F | Reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Balz-Schiemann Reaction

similar_existing.transformation_type: ortho formylation of phenols and heteroarenes via dichlorocarbene chemistry
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational dichlorocarbene-based formylation method for phenols and related heteroarenes that usually delivers ortho-aldehydes but can also produce abnormal rearrangement products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or azlactone precursors to α,β-unsaturated acids or azlactones
similar_existing.description_short: Classical olefination manifold that converts aromatic aldehydes into cinnamic-acid-type products or azlactone intermediates using anhydride-based carbon nucleophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-mediated conversion of nitriles and alcohols or thiols into imino ether or imino thioether salts and downstream amidines or esters
similar_existing.description_short: Classical nitrile activation method that turns nitriles into imino ether salts and provides a versatile gateway to amidines, esters, and related nitrogen-rich motifs.

title: JOHNSON-CLAISEN REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Johnson-Claisen rearrangement describing heating allylic alcohols with trialkyl orthoacetates under weakly acidic conditions to generate ketene acetals that undergo a [3,3]-sigmatropic shift to gamma,delta-unsaturated esters.
notes: Key concepts: exchange of allylic alcohols with ortho esters, ketene acetal formation, Claisen-type rearrangement under thermal or clay/microwave activation, and diastereocontrol explained by chairlike transition states with 1,3-diaxial interactions.

entities.notes: Named orthoester Claisen family overview.
Parent [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement family from which the Johnson-Claisen variant derives.
Characteristic rearrangement product class generated from allylic alcohols and ortho esters.

similar_existing.transformation_type: thermal Claisen rearrangement of allylic alcohols to gamma,delta-unsaturated esters
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement of allylic alcohols and ortho esters that installs gamma,delta-unsaturated ester motifs with predictable alkene geometry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allyl ether to allyl carbonyl or allyl phenol [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allyl vinyl ether|allyl aryl ether|heat|Lewis acid variants
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds or allyl phenols.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: [3,3] | 1. LHMDS, toluene, -78 °C; 2. 120 °C, 6h | CH3CH2COCl; K2CO3, Me3Al; CHCl3, 0 °C
similar_existing.description_short: Amide enolate induced aza-Claisen rearrangement in the synthesis of fluvircinine A1

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of activated hydroxamic acids to isocyanates and trapped amine/urea/carbamate products
similar_existing.description_short: Activated-hydroxamate rearrangement that furnishes isocyanates under mild conditions and enables access to amines, carbamates, and ureas after trapping.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allylic alcohol to gamma,delta-unsaturated amide rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement that converts allylic alcohols into gamma,delta-unsaturated amides under thermal conditions using DMA-DMA.

title: JONES OXIDATION / OXIDATION OF ALCOHOLS BY CHROMIUM REAGENTS
summary: Overview page for the Jones oxidation describing chromium trioxide or dichromate in aqueous sulfuric acid/acetone and related chromium(VI) reagents that oxidize primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes, ketones, or carboxylic acids.
notes: Key concepts: chromate ester formation, Jones reagent conditions, overoxidation of primary alcohols, adaptation to Sarett/Collins oxidations in nonaqueous media, and comparison with PCC or PDC for milder oxidation of alcohols and oximes.

entities.notes: Chromium(VI) oxidation family overview.
Related nonaqueous Cr(VI) oxidation variant compared on the overview page alongside Sarett, PCC, and PDC oxidations.
Representative oxidation product class from secondary alcohols under Jones or related chromium-reagent conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aluminum-alkoxide-mediated oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones with a carbonyl hydride acceptor
similar_existing.description_short: Transfer-hydrogen oxidation method that converts alcohols into carbonyl compounds under mild aluminum alkoxide conditions and is the formal reverse of the MPV reduction.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-mediated dehydrogenation of silyl enol ethers to enones and enals
similar_existing.description_short: Named oxidation that converts silyl enol ethers into conjugated enones or enals under Pd(II) conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

title: JULIA-LYTHGOE OLEFINATION
summary: Overview page for the Julia-Lythgoe olefination describing the addition of alpha-metallated phenyl sulfones to aldehydes or ketones, acylation of the resulting beta-alkoxysulfones, and reductive fragmentation to predominantly (E)-alkenes.
notes: Key concepts: classical multi-step Julia-Lythgoe sequence, one-pot heteroaryl sulfone variants, Smiles-type rearrangement of benzothiazolyl sulfones, Na(Hg) or SmI2 mediated reductive elimination, and Kocienski-modified selectivity trends.

entities.notes: Sulfone olefination family overview.
One-pot heteroaryl sulfone variant highlighted on the overview page.
Characteristic major alkene geometry delivered by the classical Julia-Lythgoe sequence.

similar_existing.transformation_type: sulfone-based olefination to predominantly E-alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified sulfone olefination strategies that transform carbonyl compounds into stereodefined alkenes, often favoring E geometry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: olefination of carbonyl compounds via α-silyl carbanions and β-hydroxysilane elimination
similar_existing.description_short: Silicon-based olefination method that turns carbonyl compounds into alkenes through β-hydroxysilane intermediates with condition-dependent E/Z control.

similar_existing.transformation_type: vicinal diol deoxygenation to alkene
similar_existing.description_short: Stereospecific deoxygenation of vicinal diols to alkenes through cyclic thionocarbonate intermediates and phosphite-triggered elimination.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

family_name_norm: kagan molander samarium diiodide mediated coupling
title: KAGAN-MOLANDER SAMARIUM DIIODIDE-MEDIATED COUPLING
summary: Overview page for Kagan-Molander samarium diiodide-mediated coupling describing intermolecular and intramolecular SmI2-promoted reactions of alkyl halides or tosylates with aldehydes and ketones, together with acyl substitutions and tandem couplings to cyclic ketones or bicyclic alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: samarium Grignard and samarium Barbier manifolds, HMPA or DMPU co-solvent acceleration, NiI2/Fe(DBM)3/copper additives, nucleophilic acyl substitution of halo esters, and tandem coupling sequences that generate complex polycycles.

entities.notes: SmI2-mediated coupling family overview.
The overview explicitly situates Kagan-Molander chemistry within samarium Barbier and samarium Grignard manifolds.
Representative product class obtained from intramolecular SmI2-mediated coupling of halo carbonyl precursors.

similar_existing.transformation_type: SmI2-mediated coupling of alkyl halides or halo carbonyl derivatives with carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Samarium diiodide-based coupling platform that enables intermolecular and intramolecular Barbier, Grignard, Reformatsky, and acyl-substitution processes in complex synthesis.

title: KAHNE GLYCOSIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Kahne glycosidation describing activation of glycosyl sulfoxides with triflic anhydride or related activators to generate glycosyl triflates that react with O-, S-, or N-nucleophiles with high anomeric control.
notes: Key concepts: sulfoxide method; glycosyl triflate formation; activation by Tf2O, TMSOTf, or TfOH with bases such as DTBMP/TTBP; participation of neighboring groups; and low-temperature coupling to alcohol, phenol, thiol, or amide nucleophiles.

entities.notes: Sulfoxide-based glycosylation family overview.
Key reactive intermediate formed upon activation of glycosyl sulfoxides.
Classical glycosidation method contrasted with the Kahne sulfoxide strategy.

similar_existing.transformation_type: glycosylation via glycosyl sulfoxide activation to glycosyl triflates followed by stereocontrolled O-, S-, or N-glycoside formation
similar_existing.description_short: Sulfoxide-based glycosylation method that activates glycosyl sulfoxides to glycosyl triflates and couples them with hindered O- or N-nucleophiles with high stereocontrol.

similar_existing.transformation_type: glycosidation of glycosyl halides with alcohols or phenols under heavy-metal salts or Lewis-acid activation
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational glycosidation method using glycosyl halides and metal-salt or Lewis-acid promoters to form O-glycosides with substrate- and neighboring-group-controlled stereochemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.

title: KECK ASYMMETRIC ALLYLATION
summary: Overview page for the Keck asymmetric allylation describing catalytic enantioselective addition of allylstannanes or allylsilanes to aldehydes under BINOL/Ti(IV) Lewis-acid catalysis to form chiral secondary homoallylic alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: in situ assembly of Ti(Oi-Pr)4/BINOL catalysts, use of molecular sieves, aldehyde allylation with allyltributylstannane, enantioselective transition-state models, and catalyst variants derived from BINOL and diamine ligands.

entities.notes: Catalytic enantioselective allylation family overview.
BINOL/Ti(IV) catalyst variant highlighted as a precursor to the Keck allylation platform.
Characteristic chiral alcohol product formed by aldehyde allylation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enantioselective allylation of aldehydes to homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes or allylsilanes under chiral Lewis-acid catalysis
similar_existing.description_short: Catalytic enantioselective allylation platform that converts aldehydes into chiral homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes and BINOL/Ti(IV) Lewis-acid catalysts.

similar_existing.transformation_type: diastereoselective alpha-alkylation of pseudoephedrine-derived amide enolates
similar_existing.description_short: Chiral auxiliary-based alkylation method that enables highly selective construction of alpha-substituted carboxylic acid derivatives.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enantioselective allylboration and crotylboration of aldehydes
similar_existing.description_short: Tartrate-controlled allylboration method for preparing homoallylic alcohols with high enantio- and diastereoselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical allyl transfer from allyltributyltin to alkyl halides or related radical precursors
similar_existing.description_short: Free-radical allylation method that replaces halides or related radical leaving groups with allyl fragments using allyltributyltin under chain conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enantioselective dioxirane epoxidation of unfunctionalized alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Chiral ketone-catalyzed epoxidation that uses Oxone-generated dioxiranes to furnish enantioenriched epoxides from alkenes.

title: KECK MACROLACTONIZATION
summary: Overview page for the Keck macrolactonization describing high-dilution cyclization of hydroxy acids using carbodiimide activation with DMAP and DMAP·HCl or EDCI to form medium- and large-ring lactones.
notes: Key concepts: syringe-pump substrate addition under reflux, DCC or EDCI activation, DMAP/DMAP·HCl proton-transfer mediation, suppression of N-acylurea byproducts, and comparison with Corey-Nicolaou and Shiina-type macrolactonizations.

entities.notes: Macrolactonization family overview.
Earlier macrolactonization protocol explicitly contrasted with the Keck modification.
Characteristic macrocyclic product class generated from hydroxy-acid precursors.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium- and large-ring lactones using carbodiimide activation with DMAP/DMAP.HCl or EDCI
similar_existing.description_short: Macrolactonization protocol for cyclizing hydroxy acids to macrocycles using carbodiimide activation and DMAP/DMAP.HCl mediation under high-dilution conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: high-dilution macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium or large ring lactones
similar_existing.description_short: Macrocyclization method that converts hydroxy acids into medium- and large-ring lactones via mixed anhydrides and DMAP-catalyzed intramolecular acyl transfer.

similar_existing.transformation_type: hydroxy acid cyclization to medium- or large-ring lactone
similar_existing.description_short: Macrolactonization method that activates hydroxy acids as 2-pyridinethiol esters before intramolecular closure to medium- and large-ring lactones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical allyl transfer from allyltributyltin to alkyl halides or related radical precursors
similar_existing.description_short: Free-radical allylation method that replaces halides or related radical leaving groups with allyl fragments using allyltributyltin under chain conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters
similar_existing.description_short: Intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters that yields cyclic beta-keto esters, often complemented by related nitrile cyclizations in ring-building synthesis.

title: KECK RADICAL ALLYLATION
summary: Overview page for the Keck radical allylation describing free-radical substitution of alkyl halides and related radical precursors with allyltributyltin under AIBN, benzoyl peroxide, heat, or photolysis to install allyl groups.
notes: Key concepts: atom-transfer initiation from alkyl bromides, chlorides, or selenides; radical chain propagation with allyltributyltin; chemoselective allylation of halides over many functional groups; and mechanistic comparison with earlier Kosugi and Grignon procedures.

entities.notes: Radical allylation family overview.
Key allyl donor used in tin-mediated radical chain transfer.
Representative radical precursor class for allyl substitution under Keck conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical allyl transfer from allyltributyltin to alkyl halides or related radical precursors
similar_existing.description_short: Free-radical allylation method that replaces halides or related radical leaving groups with allyl fragments using allyltributyltin under chain conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enantioselective allylation of aldehydes to homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes or allylsilanes under chiral Lewis-acid catalysis
similar_existing.description_short: Catalytic enantioselective allylation platform that converts aldehydes into chiral homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes and BINOL/Ti(IV) Lewis-acid catalysts.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted allylation of carbonyl compounds with allylsilanes
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon–carbon bond-forming allylation of carbonyl compounds using allylsilanes under Lewis-acid activation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational palladium-catalyzed coupling of unsaturated halides or pseudohalides with olefins to form substituted alkenes.

title: KNOEVENAGEL CONDENSATION
summary: Overview page for the Knoevenagel condensation describing base- or amine-catalyzed condensation of aldehydes or ketones with active methylene compounds to furnish alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl or related electron-poor alkenes.
notes: Key concepts: primary, secondary, or tertiary amine catalysis; active methylene donors such as malonates, cyanoacetates, or 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds; dehydration of beta-hydroxyl intermediates; and competition between aldol addition and iminium/enamine pathways.

entities.notes: Activated-methylene condensation family overview.
Classic mechanistic proposal describing amine-catalyzed formation of alpha,beta-unsaturated products.
Characteristic electron-poor alkene product generated from carbonyl and active methylene partners.

similar_existing.transformation_type: amine- or base-catalyzed condensation of aldehydes or ketones with active methylene compounds to electron-poor alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Condensation of carbonyl compounds with active methylene donors under basic or amine catalysis to form alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl derivatives and related activated alkenes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-mediated condensation of carbonyl compounds with dialkyl succinates
similar_existing.description_short: Condensation of aldehydes or ketones with dialkyl succinates to furnish alkylidene succinic half-esters after acidification.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters
similar_existing.description_short: Intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters that yields cyclic beta-keto esters, often complemented by related nitrile cyclizations in ring-building synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: LDA, THF | reagent; NaCN, DMSO | reagent | NaOEt, THF / EtOH | reagent; Me3SiOK, THF, then HCl | reagent; BH3·SMe2, THF, then EtOH/HCl | reagent; NaH, LiBH4, 1,4-dioxane, then 0.5 M HCl | reagent | LDA, THF, HMPA, ZnCl2-ether | reagent; CSA, CH(OMe)3, MeOH, CH2Cl2 | reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Mixed Claisen Condensation

similar_existing.transformation_type: ester reductive dimerization to α-hydroxy ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Acyloin condensation reductively couples esters to acyloins and is especially valuable for cyclic and intramolecular acyloin formation.

title: KNORR PYRROLE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Knorr pyrrole synthesis describing condensation of alpha-amino ketones with ketones or active methylene compounds to furnish substituted pyrroles, often after in situ reduction of alpha-nitroso ketones.
notes: Key concepts: preparation of alpha-amino ketones from alpha-nitroso ketones under Zn/AcOH, sodium dithionite, or hydrogenation conditions; condensation with 1,3-dicarbonyl or other activated carbonyl partners; imine/enamine cyclization; and regiochemical control in substituted pyrrole formation.

entities.notes: Condensation-based pyrrole synthesis family overview.
Characteristic heteroaromatic product class generated from alpha-amino ketones and carbonyl partners.
Mentioned as an alternative route to the required alpha-amino ketone substrate class.

similar_existing.transformation_type: condensation of alpha-amino ketones with ketones or active methylene compounds to substituted pyrroles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical pyrrole synthesis that converts alpha-amino ketones and carbonyl partners into highly substituted pyrroles through condensation, enamine formation, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: condensation of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds with ammonia or primary amines to substituted pyrroles
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational pyrrole-forming condensation that converts 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds and ammonia equivalents or amines into substituted pyrroles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: cyclodehydration of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds or surrogates to substituted furans
similar_existing.description_short: Classical heterocycle-forming reaction that transforms 1,4-dicarbonyl systems into furans by acid- or dehydrating-agent-mediated cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulation of acylmethylpyridinium salts with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to substituted pyridines
similar_existing.description_short: Pyridine-annulation method that converts acylmethylpyridinium salts and enones into substituted pyridines through Michael addition, diketone formation, and cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

title: KOENIGS-KNORR GLYCOSIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Koenigs-Knorr glycosidation describing coupling of glycosyl halides with alcohols or phenols under heavy-metal salts, Lewis acids, or phase-transfer conditions to form O-alkyl or aryl glycosides with substrate-controlled stereochemistry.
notes: Key concepts: preparation and activation of glycosyl chlorides or bromides; neighboring-group participation for beta-selectivity; use of silver or mercury salts and Lewis acids; oxocarbenium/dioxolanium ion reactivity; and limitations caused by elimination or hydrolysis of glycosyl halides.

entities.notes: Classical glycosyl-halide coupling family overview.
Reactive cationic intermediate invoked during activation of glycosyl halides.
Representative donor class activated by heavy metal salts or Lewis acids in the Koenigs-Knorr reaction.

similar_existing.transformation_type: glycosidation of glycosyl halides with alcohols or phenols under heavy-metal salts or Lewis-acid activation
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational glycosidation method using glycosyl halides and metal-salt or Lewis-acid promoters to form O-glycosides with substrate- and neighboring-group-controlled stereochemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: glycosylation via glycosyl sulfoxide activation to glycosyl triflates followed by stereocontrolled O-, S-, or N-glycoside formation
similar_existing.description_short: Sulfoxide-based glycosylation method that activates glycosyl sulfoxides to glycosyl triflates and couples them with hindered O- or N-nucleophiles with high stereocontrol.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aluminum-alkoxide-mediated oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones with a carbonyl hydride acceptor
similar_existing.description_short: Transfer-hydrogen oxidation method that converts alcohols into carbonyl compounds under mild aluminum alkoxide conditions and is the formal reverse of the MPV reduction.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.

title: KOLBE-SCHMITT REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Kolbe-Schmitt reaction describing carboxylation of phenoxide salts with carbon dioxide under thermal or pressurized conditions to afford ortho- or para-hydroxy aromatic carboxylic acids such as salicylic acid derivatives.
notes: Key concepts: conversion of phenols to alkali-metal phenoxides, CO2 insertion under heat or pressure, regiochemical control by cation size and substrate substitution, phenoxide-CO2 complex formation, and application to mono- or polyhydric phenols and naphthols.

entities.notes: Aromatic phenoxide carboxylation family overview.
Historical prototype product demonstrating ortho carboxylation of sodium phenoxide by carbon dioxide.
Reactive complex proposed to mediate thermal carboxylation of alkali-metal phenoxides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: carboxylation of phenoxide salts with carbon dioxide to salicylic-acid-type aromatic hydroxy acids
similar_existing.description_short: Aromatic carboxylation method that transforms phenoxides into salicylic-acid or para-hydroxybenzoic-acid derivatives under CO2, heat, and base.

similar_existing.transformation_type: hydrazoic-acid-mediated rearrangement of carbonyl compounds and carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Azide-based rearrangement that converts carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids into nitrogen-containing products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nitrile-based aromatic acylation to aryl ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Electrophilic aromatic acylation that converts activated nitriles and electron-rich arenes into aromatic ketones after hydrolysis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alkene -> cyclopropane
similar_existing.description_short: Color-explanation page family seed from page xiv.

title: KORNBLUM OXIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Kornblum oxidation describing conversion of activated alkyl halides or alpha-halo carbonyl compounds to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha-oxo carbonyl compounds by dimethyl sulfoxide under basic or silver-assisted conditions.
notes: Key concepts: displacement of halide by DMSO to form alkoxysulfonium salts, deprotonation and [2,3]-sigmatropic or elimination pathways to carbonyl compounds, silver-assisted activation with AgOTs or AgBF4, and Ganem variants employing tertiary amine N-oxides.

entities.notes: DMSO-mediated oxidation family overview.
Milder N-oxide-mediated variant contrasted with silver-assisted and classical Kornblum conditions.
Key DMSO-derived intermediate en route to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha-oxo carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidation of activated alkyl halides or alpha-halo carbonyl compounds to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha-oxo carbonyl compounds by DMSO
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation platform that converts activated halides into carbonyl compounds through DMSO-derived alkoxysulfonium intermediates under basic, silver-assisted, or N-oxide-mediated conditions.

similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alcohol oxidation to aldehydes or ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Low-temperature oxidation of alcohols with DMSO activated by oxalyl chloride or related reagents to give aldehydes or ketones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.

title: KRAPCHO DEALKOXYCARBONYLATION (KRAPCHO REACTION)
summary: Overview page for the Krapcho dealkoxycarbonylation describing salt-promoted cleavage of methoxycarbonyl or ethoxycarbonyl groups from malonic esters, beta-keto esters, alpha-cyano esters, and related electron-poor esters in wet polar aprotic solvents.
notes: Key concepts: one-pot dealkoxycarbonylation using NaCN, KCN, LiCl, NaCl, or related salts in DMSO, DMF, DMA, or HMPT; dependence on alpha-substitution and ester identity; mechanistic pathways involving cyanide addition or SN2 displacement at alkyl esters; and compatibility with base-sensitive substrates.

entities.notes: Salt-promoted dealkoxycarbonylation family overview.
Representative substrate class that loses alkoxycarbonyl groups under Krapcho conditions.
Characteristic decarboxymethylated or decarbethoxylated product class formed from activated esters.

similar_existing.transformation_type: salt-promoted removal of alkoxycarbonyl groups from malonic, beta-keto, alpha-cyano, and related activated esters
similar_existing.description_short: Dealkoxycarbonylation method that removes methoxycarbonyl or ethoxycarbonyl groups from activated esters under neutral salt-promoted conditions in wet polar aprotic solvents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

similar_existing.description_short: Barton Reaction

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted allylation of carbonyl compounds with allylsilanes
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon–carbon bond-forming allylation of carbonyl compounds using allylsilanes under Lewis-acid activation.

title: KRÖHNKE PYRIDINE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Kröhnke pyridine synthesis describing the condensation of pyridinium salts derived from alpha-haloketones with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to furnish substituted pyridines.
notes: Key concepts: preparation of acylmethylpyridinium salts from alpha-haloketones and pyridine; Michael addition to enones; 1,5-diketone formation; cyclization with ammonium acetate or related ammonia surrogates; and direct pyridine formation without separate oxidation.

entities.notes: Pyridine-annulation family overview.
Reactive pyridinium intermediate generated from alpha-haloketones and pyridine prior to annulation.
Characteristic heteroaromatic product class formed after Michael addition and cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulation of acylmethylpyridinium salts with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to substituted pyridines
similar_existing.description_short: Pyridine-annulation method that converts acylmethylpyridinium salts and enones into substituted pyridines through Michael addition, diketone formation, and cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: condensation of alpha-amino ketones with ketones or active methylene compounds to substituted pyrroles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical pyrrole synthesis that converts alpha-amino ketones and carbonyl partners into highly substituted pyrroles through condensation, enamine formation, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: multicomponent condensation to 1,4-dihydropyridines and pyridines
similar_existing.description_short: Classical multicomponent condensation used to access 1,4-dihydropyridines and, after oxidation, substituted pyridines.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enamine-mediated alpha-functionalization of carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Use of enamines as enolate equivalents for alpha-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, and related carbonyl functionalization.

title: KULINKOVICH REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Kulinkovich reaction describing titanium-mediated conversion of esters or amides into 1-alkylcyclopropanols and cyclopropylamines using Grignard reagents and titanium alkoxides.
notes: Key concepts: generation of titanacyclopropane from Ti(O-i-Pr)4 and EtMgBr or related Grignard reagents; dialkylation of esters or amides; cis-selective cyclopropane formation; sensitivity to steric demand and substrate class; and catalytic variants using reduced titanium loadings.

entities.notes: Titanium-mediated cyclopropanation family overview.
Key organotitanium intermediate that transfers a 1,2-dicarbon equivalent to esters or amides.
Signature product class generated from esters under Kulinkovich conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: titanium-mediated conversion of esters or amides into cyclopropanols and cyclopropylamines with Grignard reagents
similar_existing.description_short: Titanium-mediated cyclopropanation of esters and amides that furnishes cyclopropanols or cyclopropylamines via titanacyclopropane intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aminomethylation of enolizable carbonyls or other CH-acidic partners with formaldehyde and amines
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational carbon–carbon bond-forming reaction that installs aminomethyl substituents onto enolizable carbonyl systems via iminium intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical substitution of protonated electron-deficient heteroarenes
similar_existing.description_short: Radical-based alkylation or acylation of electron-poor heteroarenes that provides direct C-H functionalization under oxidative acidic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acylative activation of tertiary amine N-oxides to iminium intermediates and downstream α-functionalized amine products
similar_existing.description_short: N-oxide activation chemistry that transforms tertiary amines into iminium equivalents and enables oxidative skeletal editing, fragmentation, or α-functionalization in complex alkaloid synthesis.

title: KUMADA CROSS-COUPLING
summary: Overview page for the Kumada cross-coupling describing transition-metal-catalyzed union of aryl or alkenyl halides with Grignard or organolithium reagents to form carbon-carbon bonds.
notes: Key concepts: Ni- or Pd-catalyzed oxidative addition/transmetalation/reductive elimination cycles; stereoretentive coupling of alkenyl halides; dependence of reactivity on ligand and halide type; and tradeoffs between organomagnesium and more reactive organolithium or organoaluminum partners.

entities.notes: Transition-metal-catalyzed Kumada coupling overview.
Organomagnesium coupling partner used in Ni- or Pd-catalyzed carbon-carbon bond formation.
Generic cross-coupled alkene or arene product obtained after oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Ni- or Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling of aryl or alkenyl halides with Grignard or organolithium reagents
similar_existing.description_short: Transition-metal-catalyzed cross-coupling that joins aryl or alkenyl halides with organomagnesium or related organometallic reagents to form C-C bonds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: organoboron + organohalide -> C-C bond formation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: boronic acid or boronate; Pd catalyst; base
similar_existing.description_short: Front-matter mechanism panel seed from page xv.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed C-N / C-O cross-coupling
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: Pd catalysts, BINAP, dppf, XANTPHOS, NaOtBu, Cs2CO3
similar_existing.description_short: Ligand-controlled palladium-catalyzed formation of aryl C-N and C-O bonds from aryl electrophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed coupling of terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl halides
similar_existing.description_short: Cross-coupling reaction joining terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl electrophiles under palladium and copper catalysis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd- or Ni-catalyzed cross-coupling of organozinc reagents with organic electrophiles
similar_existing.description_short: Organozinc-based cross-coupling method valued for high chemoselectivity and compatibility with sensitive functional groups.

title: LAROCK INDOLE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Larock indole synthesis describing Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to furnish 2,3-disubstituted indoles or fused indole systems.
notes: Key concepts: oxidative addition of o-iodoanilines, alkyne carbopalladation, regioselectivity governed by alkyne substituent size, chloride additives and base requirements, and modified fused-system variants employing o-iodoaniline-derived imines.

entities.notes: Pd-catalyzed indole annulation family overview.
Key aryl-halide partner that undergoes oxidative addition before alkyne insertion and annulation.
Characteristic indole product class obtained from Larock heteroannulation of alkynes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: substituted nitroarene or nitrosoarene to indole using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagent
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: vinylmagnesium bromide|alkenyl Grignard|THF|-40 to -78 C|acidic work-up|nitroarene
similar_existing.description_short: The Bartoli indole synthesis rapidly converts ortho-substituted nitroarenes into indoles or azaindoles using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed conversion of arylhydrazones into substituted indoles through tautomerization, sigmatropic rearrangement, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulative synthesis of 5-hydroxyindoles from benzoquinones and enamine partners
similar_existing.description_short: Benzoquinone-based indole annulation that furnishes substituted 5-hydroxyindoles from amino-enone or enamine partners.

title: LEY OXIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Ley oxidation describing TPAP/NMO-mediated oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones under mild nonaqueous conditions.
notes: Key concepts: catalytic ruthenate reoxidation by NMO or related co-oxidants; use of TPAP with finely ground 4A molecular sieves; mild room-temperature oxidation in CH2Cl2 or MeCN; and adaptation to sensitive multifunctional substrates on small or large scale.

entities.notes: TPAP/NMO oxidation family overview.
Common synonym highlighting use of tetrapropylammonium perruthenate as the catalytic oxidant.
Typical oxidation products formed from primary and secondary alcohols under TPAP/NMO conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alcohol oxidation to aldehydes or ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Low-temperature oxidation of alcohols with DMSO activated by oxalyl chloride or related reagents to give aldehydes or ketones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: primary/secondary alcohol oxidation to aldehyde or ketone
similar_existing.description_short: Mild oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones using the NCS/DMS/Et3N reagent system via alkoxysulfonium intermediates.
